items.body: Minnesota midsummer at TPC Twin Cities — birdie-friendly fairways, hometown energy, and the first stop after The Open on the FedExCup stretch run. World No. 1 Scheffler makes his 3M debut while Kitayama returns to defend a title he won with a weekend 60-65 tear. Three weeks left before the playoffs — every point counts.

the 3M? These pitch and putts are tough. Not a whole lot of storylines. Gonna have to dig into the bag this week just to fill out a lineup. I might take Aaron Wise. He's easily the worst golfer in the field; beyond washed. He’s gotta be maxing out the credit cards just to hang onto to any last gasp of hope. But he just tied for 3rd at the ISCO… I don't know, something to keep an eye on.

items.label: Scottie Scheffler (+250 to +350):
items.body: Clear favorite in his first 3M Open start, rolling in as World No. 1 after the year's final major.

items.label: Kurt Kitayama (+1700 to +2200):
items.body: Defending champion who closed 2025 with a record-tying 60 and a Sunday 65 to win by one here.

items.label: Maverick McNealy (+1800 to +2500):
items.body: Consistent ball-striker near the top of the board and a strong fit for a birdie-fest par-71 layout.

items.label: Hideki Matsuyama (+2200 to +2800):
items.body: Major champion whose iron play and composure travel well into a high-scoring Twin Cities week.

items.label: Keith Mitchell (+2200 to +2800):
items.body: Length off the tee and aggressive scoring profile that matches this course's birdie-heavy DNA.

items.label: Jake Knapp (+2400 to +3000):
items.body: Hot recent form with elite distance and putting — a classic TPC Twin Cities outright profile.

items.label: Jackson Koivun (+2500 to +3200):
items.body: Rising name priced inside the next tier and worth watching if the low numbers keep coming.

items.label: Tom Kim (+3000 to +3800):
items.body: World-ranked ball-striker who can go low in spurts on a course that rewards aggression.

items.label: Pierceson Coody (+3200 to +4500):
items.body: Tied for third here last year at 20 under and knows how this place sets up for a weekend charge.

items.label: Venue:
items.body: TPC Twin Cities in Blaine, Minnesota — an Arnold Palmer design with Tom Lehman consultation.

items.label: Established:
items.body: PGA Tour debut in 2019; the site previously hosted the Champions Tour 3M Championship.

items.label: Defending Champion:
items.body: Kurt Kitayama won the 2025 3M Open at 23 under, beating Sam Stevens by one.

items.label: Tradition:
items.body: Minnesota's only PGA Tour stop — a late-summer birdie bash before the FedExCup Playoffs.

items.label: Course:
items.body: TPC Twin Cities, Blaine, Minnesota.

items.label: Dates:
items.body: July 23 through July 26, 2026.

items.label: Format:
items.body: 144-player field, 72-hole stroke play with a cut after 36 holes; 500 FedExCup points to the winner.

items.label: Course Profile:
items.body: 7,431 yards, par 71 — tree-lined parkland that typically plays as a high-scoring, birdie-friendly test.

items.label: Coverage:
items.body: Golf Channel early rounds; CBS Saturday and Sunday 3-6 p.m. ET (Paramount+); PGA Tour Live on ESPN+.

items.label: Event Window:
items.body: Competition runs July 23 through July 26, 2026.
